column9: Response and recovery — ISO 22301 Clause 8.4 Business Continuity Plans directly satisfies DORA Art.10 obligation to have and maintain an ICT business continuity policy.
Business Impact Analysis (22301 Clause 8.2) underpins DORA Art.10 by identifying disruptive scenarios and recovery priorities.
Business continuity strategies and solutions (8.3) translate into DORA-required recovery plans and strategies.
Recovery (22301 Clause 8.4.5) is the operational execution of DORA Art.10 recovery objectives and capabilities.
Incident response structure (22301 Clause 8.4.2) corresponds to the DORA Art.11 ICT-related incident management process.
Warning and communication (22301 Clause 8.4.3) backs DORA Art.11 notification flows during an incident.
Exercise programme (22301 Clause 8.5) operationalises DORA Art.11 requirement that incident-handling processes be tested.
Classification and reporting of ICT-related incidents — 22301 Clause 8.2 BIA prioritisation informs DORA Art.12 classification criteria (impact, duration, reputational).
Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation (Clause 9.1) feed the DORA Art.12 classification evidence.
Digital operational resilience testing programme — 22301 Clause 8.5 Exercise programme is the closest BCMS practice; DORA Art.24 extends to technical tests and TLPT for significant entities.
ICT governance and risk management framework — 22301 Clause 5.1 Leadership and commitment applies identical Annex SL expectations to BCMS and ICT resilience.
Actions to address risks and opportunities (Clause 6.1) dovetail with DORA governance accountability for resilience risks.
ICT-related incident reporting and response — 22301 Clause 10.1 Nonconformity and corrective action covers the learning loop DORA Art.17 requires from incidents.
Reporting of major incidents to authorities — 22301 Clause 9.3 Management review documents the management-level awareness DORA expects before authority reporting.
